USA.description: The US has issued Executive Orders and DoD Directives (e.g., DoD 3000.09) requiring 'appropriate levels of human judgment' over lethal force, but hasn't fully banned LAWS.

CHN.description: China has called for a ban on the *use* of fully autonomous weapons but not their development, while heavily investing in military AI.

GBR.description: The UK opposes a preemptive ban on LAWS, arguing that existing International Humanitarian Law (IHL) is sufficient to regulate them.

AUT.description: Austria introduced the 2024 UN resolution on lethal autonomous weapons and is a leading voice advocating for a legally binding global ban.

BRA.description: Brazil actively supports a legally binding instrument to prohibit autonomous weapons that lack meaningful human control.

ISR.description: Israel is a major developer of autonomous systems (e.g., Harpy loitering munition) and opposes new international legal regulations on LAWS.

RUS.description: Russia opposes a ban on LAWS, stating that meaningful human control is a subjective concept and current international law is adequate.
